checklist.entry: Treat compatibility_date as the opt-in mechanism for Workers runtime changes.
Review compatibility_flags explicitly when Node.js compatibility, runtime behaviour or breaking runtime changes are in scope.
Do not update compatibility_date casually on production Workers without regression evidence.
Record the before and after compatibility date and flags in release notes.
Check account plan limits before assuming request volume, CPU time, environment variable count, Worker size, routes, static assets or Cron Trigger capacity.
Treat CPU time, memory, subrequest count, simultaneous open connections and log size as design constraints.
Avoid buffering large request or response bodies when streaming is possible.
Use Service Bindings for Worker-to-Worker calls instead of global fetch to another Worker on the same zone.
Record any limit assumption that depends on Free, Paid, Business or Enterprise account plan state.
